title: OpenAI discounting aggressively on multi-year committed-spend deals
insight: OpenAI is pricing GPT enterprise aggressively on 2-3 year committed-spend agreements, and multiple technology prospects this quarter have cited OpenAI's commercial terms as a reason to delay. Counter with total cost of ownership including reliability and uptime, output quality on long-context tasks, and the zero-retention and no-training data terms — not headline per-token price, which is the frame we lose.

title: Long-context and agentic tool-use reliability is the durable technical differentiator
insight: In head-to-head technical evaluations, technology prospects consistently rate Claude higher on long-document reasoning and multi-step agentic tool-use reliability. The winning play in technical validation is a prospect-data eval on their hardest long-context or agentic workflow, not a public-benchmark comparison that prospects already discount.

title: Enterprise data governance (zero retention, no training) increasingly decisive
insight: Regulated-adjacent technology buyers — fintech infrastructure, health-tech, security — increasingly require contractual zero-retention and no-training commitments before security sign-off. This is where competitor deals stall in InfoSec review. Surfacing our DPA early converts the governance gate from a deal risk into a differentiator.

title: Gemini/Vertex bundling pressure via committed GCP spend
insight: Prospects with large committed Google Cloud spend are being steered toward Gemini through committed-use discounts and Vertex bundling. Contrast on model quality, steerability, and cross-cloud portability — competing on bundled price is structurally unwinnable against credits the customer has already paid for.

title: Open-weight self-host narrative used as a budget anchor
insight: Engineering-led buyers increasingly float self-hosting open-weight models (Llama, Mistral) as a cost-control lever. Reframe on total cost of ownership — inference infrastructure, eval and safety maintenance, and the engineering hours to keep a self-hosted stack current — rather than dismissing the option, which reads as defensive.
